syntaxReference: Substring: String sub = s.substring( start, end ); // end exclusive
Search: int i = s.indexOf("an"); int j = s.lastIndexOf("an");
Compare: boolean eq = s.equals(t); boolean ci = s.equalsIgnoreCase(t);
StringBuilder: StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder(); sb.append(x); String res = sb.toString();
commonPatterns:
Split on whitespace:
String[] tokens = s.trim().split("\\s+");

Safe numeric concat:
String line = "Count=" + count; // or String.format("Count=%d", count)
quickTips: Use equals, not ==, for string content comparison.
Remember substring end index is exclusive; watch for off-by-one errors.
Prefer StringBuilder for heavy concatenation inside loops.
